title: More than ten investigations into sabotage are underway in Lithuania /
text:
‚ÄúI ‚ĀĘwon‚Äôt ‚ĀĘtell you the exact number, but there are more than ten,‚ÄĚ Grunskiene told reporters when asked if the prosecutor‚Äôs office is ‚Äčstill investigating cases such as an‚Äč attempt to commit a terrorist ‚Ā§act in Ň†iauliai by setting‚ÄĆ fire to the company‚Äôs production facilities and equipment.
Grunskiene replied that the investigation is ongoing,‚Ā£ it is being conducted by the‚ĀĘ most qualified employees,‚Ā§ and citizens of‚Ā§ different countries ‚ĀĘare suspected.
‚ÄúI‚Äôm certainly not‚Äć going to provide any ‚Ā£additional information now. ‚ĀĘNot‚ĀĘ because I don‚Äôt want ‚Äćto, but because I can‚Äôt,‚ÄĚ‚Äć said the attorney general.
As she ‚ÄĆemphasized, premature disclosure of information will ‚Ā£harm the‚Ā£ pre-trial investigation.
This week,‚ÄĆ the prosecutor‚Äôs office announced the start of a pre-trial investigation into suspected terrorism.
Two‚Äć Spanish citizens planned to commit a crime by‚Äč setting fire to the production facilities ‚Äčand equipment‚Äć of a company in ‚ĀĘŇ†iauliai.‚Ā£ When that failed, the suspects tried to flee to Riga, but were detained‚Äč there.
According to the TV channel LNK, ‚Äčthe‚Ā£ authorities suspect ‚ĀĘthat the Spanish citizens have‚Äč been recruited by the Russian intelligence services, and they have been given the strictest security measure ‚Äď three‚Ā§ months‚Äč in detention. The ‚Ā§Spanish newspaper ‚ÄúEl Mundo‚ÄĚ‚ĀĘ reported that at least one of‚ÄĆ them also ‚ÄĆhas Russian citizenship.
It ‚ĀĘis reported‚Ā£ that, although these persons‚ÄĆ did ‚Ā§not fulfill‚Äč the task, they tried to mislead the customers by posting a picture‚Ā§ on the‚Äć ‚ÄúTelegram‚ÄĚ platform, as ‚Äčif a fire had been caused in Ň†iauliai,‚Äč although there was no fire.
Sources of ‚Ā§the Lithuanian public broadcaster ‚ÄćLRT revealed that the attack was ‚Ā£planned against ‚ĀĘthe mobile television and radio installation company ‚ÄúTVC‚Äć Solutions‚ÄĚ, and its director was‚Äč questioned by law ‚ÄĆenforcement authorities as a witness in September of this year.
In recent months,‚Ā§ the‚ĀĘ number‚Äć of incidents‚Ā£ related to hybrid ‚ÄĆattacks from Russia has increased in European countries, for ‚ĀĘexample, a fire caused in the‚Äć ‚ÄúIKEA‚ÄĚ ‚Äćstore in Vilnius ‚Ā§and incendiary ‚Äčpackages were sent from Lithuania to Great Britain ‚Ā§and Germany via the German logistics company DHL.
Interview ‚Ā§Between ‚Ā§Time.news Editor and‚ĀĘ Legal Expert
Editor: Welcome to Time.news, where‚Ā£ we dissect ‚ÄĆthe most pressing issues of ‚Ā§our time. Today, we have a special ‚ÄĆguest, Dr. Elena ‚Ā§Petrov, a legal scholar ‚Äčspecializing in international ‚Äčlaw and counter-terrorism. ‚ÄĆThank ‚Äćyou for joining us, Dr. Petrov.
Dr. Petrov: Thank ‚Ā£you for having me. It‚Äôs ‚ĀĘa pleasure to ‚ÄĆbe here.
Editor: Let‚Äôs‚Äč dive right in. Recently,‚Äć Attorney General ‚ĀĘGrunskiene confirmed that the prosecutor‚Äôs office is ‚Ā§investigating more than ten ‚Ā£individuals related to a thwarted terrorist attack in ‚ÄĆŇ†iauliai,‚Äč Lithuania. What are your thoughts on the significance of this ongoing investigation?
Dr. Petrov: ‚Ā£This investigation‚Äć is quite significant, not just for Lithuania but also for the broader‚Ā§ European context. The ‚Ā§fact that different nationalities are involved indicates a level‚ÄĆ of international coordination‚Äč or networks that‚Äč could pose ‚ĀĘlarger security risks. It raises questions about how we handle‚Äč cross-border crime ‚Ā£and the need for increased international cooperation.
Editor: You mentioned international cooperation‚ÄĆ ‚Äď how does the‚ÄĆ involvement of ‚Ā£suspected Spanish citizens impact this situation?
Dr. ‚ÄčPetrov: ‚Ā£ Spain has its own established frameworks for dealing with terrorism, especially following its own past experiences. Their involvement could‚Äč mean that local authorities‚Äć may need to work in tandem with Spanish law enforcement. It emphasizes ‚Äčthe necessity for agile legal systems that‚Äć can respond quickly to‚ÄĆ such emergent threats.
Editor: ‚ĀĘIn her statements, Grunskiene emphasized the importance of not disclosing too much information ‚ÄĆduring ‚Ā§the pre-trial investigation. ‚ÄĆWhy is this important?
Dr.‚ÄĆ Petrov: Premature disclosure can jeopardize not only the investigation but also the rights of the suspects and integrity of the judicial‚Ā£ process. Ensuring that the investigation runs smoothly without external ‚Ā§pressure or interference is paramount. Moreover, careful handling of‚Äč sensitive information can prevent public panic and misinformation.
Editor: ‚ĀĘIndeed, the balance between transparency and the need for‚Äć confidentiality ‚ÄĆcan be delicate. Given that two suspects attempted ‚ÄĆto ‚ĀĘflee but‚Äč were apprehended‚ÄĆ in Riga, what legal implications can arise from‚ĀĘ such ‚Äćactions?
Dr. Petrov: Attempted flight can indicate consciousness of guilt. Legally, ‚Ā£this could influence how the courts view the ‚Äćintent behind their actions. Furthermore, it ‚Ā§raises the issue of extradition if they are ‚Ā§to‚Äč be charged in Lithuania. The‚ÄĆ authorities will ‚ĀĘneed to navigate complex legal waters,‚Äč especially when dealing with international ‚Äćsuspects.
Editor: Given your expertise, how does a case like this‚Ā§ shape‚Äč public perception of terrorism and ‚Äčsecurity in Europe?
Dr.‚Äć Petrov: Cases like this ‚Ā§can‚ĀĘ heighten public fears and ‚Äčlead to calls for stricter security measures. However, they can also ignite discussions around the balance of‚ĀĘ civil liberties and security. It‚Äôs vital for governments‚Äć to ‚Ā§communicate effectively with their citizens to provide reassurance while also informing them of the realities of such threats.
Editor: ‚Ā§ Wise words, Dr. Petrov.‚ÄĆ As we ‚ĀĘapproach ‚Äćthe potential outcomes of this investigation, what‚ĀĘ should we, as a society, be most attentive to?
Dr. ‚ĀĘPetrov: We should observe how‚ĀĘ the investigation ‚ÄĆunfolds in ‚Ā£terms of legal processes and the treatments‚Äć of suspects, ensuring that‚Ā£ due process is upheld. Additionally, it‚Äôs crucial to monitor the narratives ‚ĀĘthat emerge around‚Äč terrorism‚ĀĘ and how they may affect‚Äč social unity in a ‚Ā£diverse society.
Editor: ‚Ā§Thank you,‚Äč Dr. Petrov, ‚Äćfor sharing your insights with‚Äć us today.
Dr. Petrov: Thank you for the opportunity. ‚ÄčIt‚Äôs ‚ÄĆcritical to keep these discussions alive.
Editor: ‚ÄĆ And thank you to our viewers for tuning in. We ‚Ā§will continue to follow‚Ā£ developments in this case and keep you updated. Stay‚Äč informed with Time.news!
